title: Long-Term Residence
description: 5 years of continuous residence, a Slovak language exam, and what makes this different from unlimited permanent residence

exercises.title: Key Terms
exercises.subtitle: These are the terms you will see on forms and hear at the Foreign Police.

scenarios.situation: You have lived legally in Slovakia for 4 years and 8 months without interruption. Can you apply for long-term residence now?
choices: Yes — close enough to 5 years
No — you need 5 full years of continuous legal residence immediately before applying
Yes — if you also have a valid work contract
No — you must first get 5-year permanent residence
scenarios.answer: No — you need 5 full years of continuous legal residence immediately before applying
scenarios.answerMeaning: The condition is exactly 5 years of continuous legal residence immediately before submitting the application. 4 years and 8 months does not qualify. Wait until the 5-year mark.

scenarios.situation: What is the key requirement that long-term residence (dlhodobý pobyt) has that unlimited permanent residence (trvalý pobyt na neobmedzený čas) does not?
choices: A medical certificate from an approved clinic
Proof of accommodation
A Slovak language exam at A2 level
A criminal record from your home country
scenarios.answer: A Slovak language exam at A2 level
scenarios.answerMeaning: Since 15 July 2025, applicants for long-term residence must prove Slovak language proficiency at A2 level by passing an exam at an approved language school. This requirement does not apply to unlimited permanent residence — a key difference many people miss.

scenarios.situation: You are currently in Slovakia on a temporary residence permit for study purposes. Can you apply for long-term residence?
choices: Yes — any legal residence counts
No — temporary residence for study purposes is excluded from long-term residence eligibility
Yes — but only after finishing your degree
No — students must apply for unlimited permanent residence instead
scenarios.answer: No — temporary residence for study purposes is excluded from long-term residence eligibility
scenarios.answerMeaning: The law explicitly lists categories that cannot apply for long-term residence. Temporary residence for study purposes (prechodný pobyt na účel štúdia) is one of them. Other excluded categories include asylum seekers, seasonal workers, posted workers, and persons with diplomatic immunity.

scenarios.situation: Where do you submit the application for long-term residence?
choices: At the Slovak embassy in your home country
At any police station in Slovakia
Only at the Foreign Police in Slovakia
By post to the Ministry of Interior
scenarios.answer: Only at the Foreign Police in Slovakia
scenarios.answerMeaning: Like unlimited permanent residence, the application for long-term residence can only be submitted in person at the Foreign Police in Slovakia. You cannot apply at a Slovak embassy abroad.

scenarios.situation: You are 13 years old. Your family is applying for long-term residence. Do you need to pass the Slovak A2 language exam?
choices: Yes — the exam is required for all applicants
No — the language exam is not required for applicants under 14
Yes — but only a simplified version
No — children never need language exams
scenarios.answer: No — the language exam is not required for applicants under 14
scenarios.answerMeaning: The Slovak A2 language exam is not required if the applicant has not yet reached 14 years of age at the time of submitting the application. Other exemptions include Slovaks living abroad, and those who completed education in Slovak language.

exercises.title: At the Foreign Police — Key Phrases
exercises.subtitle: What you will say and hear at your long-term residence appointment.

rows.label: Opening
rows.slovak: Chcel/a by som požiadať o dlhodobý pobyt.
rows.english: I would like to apply for long-term residence.

rows.label: Your stay
rows.slovak: Žijem na Slovensku päť rokov nepretržite.
rows.english: I have lived in Slovakia continuously for five years.

rows.label: Language proof
rows.slovak: Tu je certifikát z jazykovej skúšky.
rows.english: Here is the certificate from the language exam.

rows.label: Money proof
rows.english: Here is the confirmation of income.

rows.label: Insurance
rows.slovak: Tu je potvrdenie o zdravotnom poistení.
rows.english: Here is the proof of health insurance.

rows.label: Timeline
rows.english: When will the decision be ready?

rows.label: Not clear
rows.english: I don't understand. Can you write it down?

words.slovak: Chcel by som požiadať o dlhodobý pobyt.
words.english: I would like to apply for long-term residence.

words.slovak: Žijem na Slovensku päť rokov nepretržite.
words.english: I have lived in Slovakia continuously for five years.

words.slovak: Tu je certifikát z jazykovej skúšky.
words.english: Here is the certificate from the language exam.
Here is the confirmation of income.

items.sentence: Na dlhodobý pobyt musím žiť na Slovensku ___ rokov nepretržite.
items.translation: For long-term residence I must live in Slovakia continuously for ___ years.
choices: tri
štyri
päť
desať

items.sentence: Od 15. júla 2025 sa vyžaduje ovládanie slovenského jazyka na úrovni ___.
items.translation: From 15 July 2025 Slovak language proficiency at level ___ is required.
choices: A1
A2
B1
B2

items.translation: The language exam is taken at a ___.
choices: cudzineckej polícii
zastupiteľskom úrade
jazykovej škole
ministerstve vnútra

items.sentence: Bez platného cestovného dokladu policajný útvar žiadosť ___.
items.translation: Without a valid travel document the Foreign Police will ___ the application.
choices: prijme
odloží
neprijme
schváli

items.sentence: Doklady priložené k žiadosti nesmú byť staršie ako ___ dní.
items.translation: Documents attached to the application must not be older than ___ days.

items.sentence: Policajný útvar rozhodne o žiadosti do ___ dní.
items.translation: The Foreign Police decides on the application within ___ days.

scenarios.situation: You studied for 3 years at a Slovak-language university and graduated 3 years ago. Do you need to take the A2 language exam?
choices: Yes — the exam is always required
No — but only if you studied at least 2 years at a Slovak-language school within the last 5 years
No — any university degree in Slovakia exempts you
Yes — university study does not count as language proof
scenarios.answer: No — but only if you studied at least 2 years at a Slovak-language school within the last 5 years
scenarios.answerMeaning: You are exempt if you studied at least 2 school years at a Slovak-language secondary or university programme and no more than 5 years have passed since finishing. You graduated 3 years ago — within the 5-year window. No exam needed.

scenarios.situation: How can you prove stable and regular financial resources?
choices: Only a bank statement is accepted
Employment contract, employer salary confirmation, bank statement, or pension document
Only an employment contract from a Slovak employer
A letter from a friend guaranteeing financial support
scenarios.answer: Employment contract, employer salary confirmation, bank statement, or pension document
scenarios.answerMeaning: Stable and regular financial resources (stabilné a pravidelné zdroje) can be proven by: an employment contract, employer confirmation of salary, a bank account statement in your name, or proof of receiving a pension. The key is that the resources are stable and regular — not a one-off amount.

scenarios.situation: Your previous long-term residence was cancelled. You want to reapply. Do you need to provide proof of accommodation this time?
choices: No — accommodation proof is never required for long-term residence
Yes — accommodation proof is required specifically when reapplying after cancellation
Only if you moved to a new address
No — only proof of finances is needed
scenarios.answer: Yes — accommodation proof is required specifically when reapplying after cancellation
scenarios.answerMeaning: Proof of accommodation is only required for long-term residence in one specific case: when the applicant is reapplying because their previous long-term residence was cancelled or lapsed for legal reasons. In all other cases it is not required.

scenarios.situation: You spent 3 years working in Slovakia and 2 years in Germany on an EU Blue Card. Can you apply for Slovak long-term residence?
choices: No — all 5 years must be in Slovakia
Yes — 5 years across Slovakia and EU member states as a Blue Card holder qualifies, with at least 2 years in Slovakia immediately before applying
Yes — but only if you worked for a Slovak company in Germany
No — Blue Card holders must apply in the country where they held the card longest
scenarios.answer: Yes — 5 years across Slovakia and EU member states as a Blue Card holder qualifies, with at least 2 years in Slovakia immediately before applying
scenarios.answerMeaning: Blue Card holders can combine residence across Slovakia and other EU member states to reach the 5-year threshold. However the last 2 years immediately before the application must be in Slovakia as a Blue Card holder or family member of one.

scenarios.situation: A colleague tells you that dlhodobý pobyt and trvalý pobyt na neobmedzený čas are basically the same thing. Is he right?
choices: Yes — they are different names for the same permit
No — the key difference is that long-term residence requires a Slovak A2 language exam; unlimited permanent residence does not
Yes — both require 5 years of residence and the same documents
No — long-term residence is only for EU citizens
scenarios.answer: No — the key difference is that long-term residence requires a Slovak A2 language exam; unlimited permanent residence does not
scenarios.answerMeaning: This is the most important distinction. Dlhodobý pobyt (long-term residence) requires a Slovak language exam at A2 level from an approved school — introduced on 15 July 2025. Trvalý pobyt na neobmedzený čas (unlimited permanent residence) does not have this requirement. They are separate permits with different qualifying routes.

exercises.context: You need to pass the Slovak A2 language exam to apply for long-term residence. You are texting the language school in Košice to find out about the exam and book a place.

A.title: Exam booked! ✓
A.description: You confirmed the exam is for long-term residence purposes and successfully booked your place.

B.title: You got all the details! ✓
B.description: You asked the right questions and know exactly what to prepare before the exam.

nodes.speaker: user
nodes.prompt: Start the conversation politely. How do you greet the school?

choices.text: Dobrý deň. Ospravedlňujem sa za vyrušenie.
choices.translation: Good day. I apologise for the disturbance.

nodes.speaker: school
nodes.text: Dobrý deň! Čím vám môžeme pomôcť?
nodes.translation: Good day! How can we help you?
nodes.wrongAnswerTranslation: Hmm... what are you talking about?

choices.text: Zaujíma ma jazyková skúška zo slovenčiny na úrovni A2.
choices.translation: I am interested in the Slovak language exam at A2 level.

choices.text: Potrebujem zložiť jazykovú skúšku pre cudzineckú políciu.
choices.translation: I need to pass a language exam for the Foreign Police.

choices.text: Chcem sa zapísať na kurz slovenčiny.
choices.translation: I want to enrol in a Slovak language course.

nodes.speaker: school
nodes.text: Rozumiem. Je to pre účely dlhodobého pobytu?
nodes.translation: I understand. Is this for the purposes of long-term residence?

choices.translation: Yes, I am applying for long-term residence.

choices.text: Áno. Potrebujem certifikát pre cudzineckú políciu.
choices.translation: Yes. I need the certificate for the Foreign Police.

nodes.speaker: school
nodes.text: Skvelé. Skúška pozostáva z písomnej a ústnej časti. Kedy by ste chceli prísť?
nodes.translation: Great. The exam consists of a written and oral part. When would you like to come?

choices.text: Čím skôr. Máte termín tento mesiac?
choices.translation: As soon as possible. Do you have a date this month?

nodes.speaker: school
nodes.text: Máme termín o dva týždne. Čo si máte pripraviť?
nodes.translation: We have a date in two weeks. What should you prepare?
nodes.wrongAnswerResponse: Prepáčte... to nie je správna odpoveď.
nodes.wrongAnswerTranslation: Sorry... that's not the right answer.

choices.text: Platný cestovný doklad a potvrdenie o pobyte.
choices.translation: A valid travel document and proof of residence.

nodes.speaker: school
nodes.text: Správne. Po úspešnom absolvovaní dostanete certifikát pre cudzineckú políciu.
nodes.translation: Correct. After successful completion you will receive a certificate for the Foreign Police.

nodes.speaker: school
nodes.translation: Would you like to register now?

choices.translation: Yes please. I would like to register.
Yes. How do I pay the fee?
No, I will think about it.

nodes.speaker: school
nodes.text: Výborne! Zapísali sme vás. Veľa šťastia na skúške! 🎓
nodes.translation: Excellent! We have registered you. Good luck on the exam! 🎓

questions.question: What does the Slovak A2 language exam consist of?
choices: Only a written test
Only an oral interview
A written and an oral part
A grammar test and a reading comprehension
questions.answer: A written and an oral part
questions.explanation: According to the language school, the A2 exam consists of a written and an oral part (písomná a ústna časť). Passing both earns you the certificate required by the Foreign Police.

questions.question: What document do you receive after passing the A2 exam?
choices: A residence permit
A certificate confirming you passed — for submission to the Foreign Police
A Slovak language diploma
An employer confirmation
questions.answer: A certificate confirming you passed — for submission to the Foreign Police
questions.explanation: After successfully completing the exam you receive a certifikát (certificate) which you then submit to the Foreign Police as part of your long-term residence application.

questions.question: Where must the Slovak A2 language exam be taken?
choices: At the Foreign Police
At any school in Slovakia
At an approved language school on the official Ministry of Interior list
Online
questions.answer: At an approved language school on the official Ministry of Interior list
questions.explanation: The exam must be taken at one of the approved language schools listed by the Ministry of Interior. Not just any school qualifies — see the reference card for approved schools in your region.

questions.question: Since when is the Slovak A2 language requirement in effect?
choices: 1 January 2024
1 January 2025
15 July 2025
It has always been required
questions.explanation: The Slovak language requirement at A2 level was introduced on 15 July 2025. Applications submitted before this date were not subject to this requirement.

scenarios.situation: You have been in Slovakia for 5 years on a seasonal work permit. Can you apply for long-term residence?
choices: Yes — 5 years of any legal residence qualifies
No — seasonal employment is specifically excluded from long-term residence eligibility
Yes — but you need a regular employment contract first
No — seasonal workers must apply for unlimited permanent residence instead
scenarios.answer: No — seasonal employment is specifically excluded from long-term residence eligibility
scenarios.answerMeaning: The law explicitly excludes several categories from applying for long-term residence. Holders of temporary residence for seasonal employment (sezónne zamestnanie) are one of them — regardless of how long they have been in Slovakia.

scenarios.situation: You completed lower secondary school (základná škola) entirely in Slovak. Do you need the A2 language exam?
choices: Yes — the exam is always required
No — completing lower secondary education at a Slovak-language school is an exemption
Yes — basic school does not count
No — but only if you also have a Slovak language certificate from somewhere
scenarios.answer: No — completing lower secondary education at a Slovak-language school is an exemption
scenarios.answerMeaning: One of the official exemptions from the A2 exam is proof that you completed lower secondary education (nižšie stredné vzdelanie) at a school with Slovak as the language of instruction. You do not need to take the exam.

scenarios.situation: Your proof of health insurance was issued 88 days ago. Your employment contract confirmation was issued 92 days ago. Which document is a problem?
choices: Both are fine
Both are a problem
Only the health insurance — 88 days is too old
Only the employment confirmation — 92 days exceeds the 90-day limit
scenarios.answer: Only the employment confirmation — 92 days exceeds the 90-day limit
scenarios.answerMeaning: The health insurance proof at 88 days is within the 90-day limit — fine. The employment confirmation at 92 days exceeds the limit — you need a fresh one from your employer.

scenarios.situation: You submit a complete long-term residence application today. After how many days must the Foreign Police give you a decision?
choices: 30 days
60 days
90 days
180 days
scenarios.answerMeaning: The Foreign Police must decide on the application within 90 days of receiving it. This is the same deadline as for other types of permanent residence.

scenarios.situation: A friend says: 'Just apply for unlimited permanent residence — it's easier than long-term residence.' What is the key reason they might be right?
choices: Unlimited permanent residence requires fewer years of residence
Unlimited permanent residence does not require a Slovak language exam
Unlimited permanent residence can be applied for at the embassy
Unlimited permanent residence has a faster decision time
scenarios.answer: Unlimited permanent residence does not require a Slovak language exam
scenarios.answerMeaning: This is the most important practical difference. If you qualify for unlimited permanent residence (through the 5-year permanent residence route), you avoid the A2 language exam requirement. Long-term residence and unlimited permanent residence are separate routes — check which one applies to your situation.

referenceCard.title: Long-Term Residence — Reference Card
referenceCard.unlockedMessage: 🎉 You completed the Long-Term Residence lesson! Your reference card is now saved to your profile.

checklist.item: Completed application form (official MV SR form)

checklist.warning: Mandatory — application not accepted without it. Bring original.

checklist.item: Proof of stable and regular financial resources
checklist.warning: Must not be older than 90 days. Accepted: employment contract, employer salary confirmation, bank statement, pension document.

checklist.item: Proof of health insurance in Slovakia
checklist.warning: Must not be older than 90 days.

checklist.item: Slovak language certificate at A2 level
checklist.slovak: Certifikát z jazykovej skúšky — úroveň A2
checklist.warning: Required from 15 July 2025. Must be from an approved language school. See school list in this card. Exemptions apply — see below.

checklist.item: Proof of accommodation (only if reapplying after cancellation)
checklist.warning: Only required if your previous long-term residence was cancelled or lapsed. Not required in other cases.

languageExemptions: Under 14 years of age at the time of application
Holds temporary residence as a Slovak living abroad
Completed lower secondary education at a Slovak-language school
Studied at least 2 school years at a Slovak-language secondary school or university — and no more than 5 years have passed since finishing
excludedCategories: Persons with temporary refuge / Odídenec status
Temporary residence for special activity (internship, EU/government programmes, international treaties)
Posted workers in international transport or service provision

keyFacts: 5 years of continuous legal residence in Slovakia immediately before applying
Slovak language at A2 level required — exam at an approved school only (since 15 July 2025)
Application only at the Foreign Police in Slovakia — not at an embassy
No valid passport = application not accepted
All supporting documents must not be older than 90 days
Key difference from unlimited permanent residence: language exam is required for long-term residence, not for unlimited permanent residence

keyPhrase.english: I would like to apply for long-term residence.
keyPhrase.slovak: Chcel/a by som požiadať o dlhodobý pobyt.

referenceCard.freeHelp: IOM Migration Information Centre: mic.iom.sk — free legal advice for foreigners
